mission.title: argo-workflows: `token not valid for running mode` after SSO authentication
mission.description: `token not valid for running mode` after SSO authentication. Requested by 28+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo-workflows deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo-workflows version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
helm list -n argo-workflows 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo-workflows installation.

steps.title: Review argo-workflows configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-workflows configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get configmap -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-workflows
```
Argo UI comes back to login page with error `{"code":16,"message":"token not valid for running mode”}` after completing SSO user authentication.

steps.title: Apply the fix for `token not valid for running mode` after SSO authentication
steps.description:
Check if Istio is interfering with the SSO token flow. When running Argo Workflows with Istio, the sidecar proxy can intercept traffic to the Kubernetes API, causing token validation failures. Add an annotation to exclude the API server IP range from Istio:
```yaml
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        traffic.sidecar.istio.io/excludeOutboundIPRanges: "<K8S_API_SERVER_IP>/32"
```
Also verify the argo-server is running in SSO mode:
```bash
argo version
kubectl get configmap workflow-controller-configmap -n argo-workflows -o yaml | grep -A 10 sso
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-workflows -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-workflows
kubectl get events -n argo-workflows --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "`token not valid for running mode` after SSO authentication" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The 'token not valid for running mode' error after SSO authentication is commonly caused by Istio's sidecar proxy intercepting traffic to the Kubernetes API. Add the traffic.sidecar.istio.io/excludeOutboundIPRanges annotation to the argo-server deployment to exclude the API server IP from Istio proxying.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-workflows installed or the issue environment reproducible.
